findings.problem: The chapter's headline design is too cleanly mirrored to feel shortlist-dangerous. Lena's race is distilled into a camera-ready ownership line, Kai enters his race under that caption, then his aftermath and the closing split-screen reframe both performances into the same thesis. Instead of preserving the asymmetry between a slower but intact swim and a catastrophic sprint override, the chapter tidies both into parallel proof texts about bodily ownership. That neatness weakens the novel's harder question rather than deepening it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Break the echo chain at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:115; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:119; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:131; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:411; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:467; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:469. Keep Lena's bronze, Kai's override, and the public misreading, but stop giving both protagonists matching verbal formulations and a merged final slogan. One viable strategy is to let one athlete's public language stay partial, evasive, or misframed while the other reaches cleaner ownership language.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised final third still contains Lena's bronze, Kai's last-place finish under his own control, and visible public misunderstanding, but no longer gives both characters parallel ownership taglines or ends on a split-screen thesis that compresses them into one motto. FAIL if Lena's screen line is still explicitly echoed through Kai's scene and the chapter still resolves on a merged caption-style statement.

findings.problem: The two mixed-zone scenes reuse nearly the same structural ladder: reporters ask whether the refusal cost the event, whether the athlete regrets it, whether the choice was political or anti-Meridian, and then push for a single sentence that will summarize the choice. Because Lena's sequence already runs that pattern in full, Kai's interview arrives partly pre-explained instead of generating a distinct new public pressure. The chapter's second half therefore repeats the framing work instead of escalating it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Re-engineer either the Lena press beat at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:65; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:71; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:97; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:103 or the Kai press beat at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:363; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:397; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:403; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:431 so the second interview turns on track-specific consequences rather than a second version of the same autonomy debate. New pressure could be medical liability, disqualification procedure, sponsor exposure, or a public safety frame unique to Kai's collapse beat.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if Kai's mixed-zone sequence introduces at least one major leverage shift or public consequence that does not already appear in Lena's mixed zone, and the order of questioning no longer repeats cost-regret-Meridian/autonomy in both scenes. FAIL if a cold reader can map the second interview onto the first with the same sequence of beats and only minor wording changes.

findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly turns pressured speech into quotable thesis lines. Lena is explicitly cornered into offering a sentence that 'would fit on a screen,' then supplies one; Kai internally rejects a 'too finished' answer, but the scene still lands on an abstract mini-speech about body and will, followed by narration certifying that another line silenced the room. That is composed dialogue in exactly the place the style bible asks for friction, and it pushes Kai especially toward movie-poster clarity rather than his more concrete, self-deflating register.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the public-answer passages at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:103; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:115; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:423; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:425; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:439; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:441. Let at least one of the key answers arrive through concrete bodily language, interruption, restart, or narrower refusal instead of a polished identity statement, and remove the narrator's certification of how the press receives the line.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised mixed-zone dialogue contains visible speech friction in the targeted exchange and no narrator sentence tells the reader that a line silenced the press or otherwise landed perfectly. FAIL if Kai still states the body-autonomy argument as a balanced maxim at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:425-equivalent or if the scene still presents a line as broadcast-ready by design.

findings.problem: After the race action has already made the chapter's meaning legible, the prose keeps translating it into abstract summary. Lena has 'watched her choose herself'; Kai's replay is explained as an image with 'no interior'; the closing monitor beat compresses the chapter into a standalone slogan. Those sentences do explanatory work the concrete event, replay footage, and public misreading had already done, so the narration starts sounding smarter and cleaner than the lived scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the explanatory passages at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:121; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:125; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:371; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:393; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:467; snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_17.md:469 so the public/private mismatch stays legible through graphics, questions, replay images, and bodily reaction rather than abstract thematic paraphrase. Trust the reader to infer the argument from the observed scene instead of naming it again after arrival.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a cold reader can infer the public misunderstanding and the athletes' private stakes from the revised monitors/reporters/body details in those passages without any abstract summary sentence about choosing selfhood, lacking interiority, or a final two-word slogan. FAIL if the revised version still restates the chapter's point after the race and mixed-zone action have already established it.

summary: The chapter lands its continuity obligations and both race set pieces carry real embodied consequence, especially the legibility of Lena's fully present swim and Kai's override at 160m. It still fails at review standard because the back half over-engineers comprehension: the two mixed-zone scenes repeat the same public framing, the dialogue keeps sharpening into camera-ready ownership lines, and the prose repeatedly paraphrases the theme after the action has already done the work.
